frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: decision bias and sensitivity dissociated for fearful-face perception

frameworks.name: perceptual confidence / metacognition
frameworks.one_line: subjective confidence indexes privileged access of fear to awareness

frameworks.name: low-level visual feature account
frameworks.one_line: fear prioritization explained solely by low-level facial features

frameworks.name: prefrontal contribution to perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: dlPFC density linked to magnitude of fear-related decision bias

core_question: Is the perceptual prioritization of fearful faces driven only by low-level features, or do higher-level decision and confidence mechanisms also contribute?
key_assumptions: low-level visual processing can be equated via masking and contrast titration
emotional intensity of faces can be matched across categories
decision bias and confidence index higher-level perceptual mechanisms
manipulations: facial emotion category (fearful vs angry vs happy)
key_findings: liberal decision bias toward perceiving fearful faces in detection and discrimination
bias is stronger in individuals with reduced dorsolateral prefrontal cortex density
confidence is higher when fearful faces are accurately perceived
interpretations: fear prioritization involves higher-level decision and confidence mechanisms, not only low-level features
fearful faces may have privileged access to consciousness
